query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "dockerless: environment-free program verifier for coding agents" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism of "code-based self-verification" for LLMs, which is the central technical contribution of the user's referenced work ("Dockerless"). Although the user's paper focuses on a "Dockerless" environment for general program verification and this paper applies the concept to math word problems, both measure the efficacy of using code execution and self-correction loops to improve LLM reliability, making it a canonical
